meta.title: Thomas Eychenne vs Umberto Zaffagnini - EPT Barcelona

solutions.name: pro
solutions.message:
Hero: Eychenne opens T8s from the CO because the hand has strong playability and can pressure the blinds.

Solver: Raise is the highest-EV action. T8s is strong enough to enter the pot aggressively 5-handed.

Avoid: Folding is too tight, while limping gives up initiative with a hand that benefits from building the pot.

solutions.name: auto
solutions.message:
Hero: Eychenne opens T8s from the CO because the hand has strong playability and can pressure the blinds.

Solver: Raise is the highest-EV action. T8s is strong enough to enter the pot aggressively 5-handed.

Avoid: Folding is too tight, while limping gives up initiative with a hand that benefits from building the pot.

solutions.name: pro
solutions.message:
Hero: Eychenne calls because T8s has good postflop playability and he will play the rest of the hand in position.

Solver: Call is the highest-EV action. The price and stack depth allow this suited connector to continue profitably.

Avoid: Folding gives up too much equity, while a 4-bet creates unnecessary variance and performs poorly against a shove.

solutions.name: auto
solutions.message:
Hero: Eychenne calls because T8s has good postflop playability and he will play the rest of the hand in position.

Solver: Call is the highest-EV action. The price and stack depth allow this suited connector to continue profitably.

Avoid: Folding gives up too much equity, while a 4-bet creates unnecessary variance and performs poorly against a shove.

solutions.name: pro
solutions.message:
Hero: Eychenne checks back with middle pair and a gutshot, keeping the pot controlled and protecting his showdown value.

Solver: Check is the highest-EV action. The hand can improve on many turns and does not need immediate protection.

Avoid: Betting can be called by stronger hands or face a check-raise, while a large bet unnecessarily turns the hand into a bluff.

solutions.name: auto

Avoid: Betting can be called by stronger hands or face a check-raise, while a large bet unnecessarily turns the hand into a bluff.

solutions.name: pro
solutions.message:
Hero: Eychenne calls with two pair, keeping bluffs in and avoiding a huge pot on a very connected board.

Solver: Call is the highest-EV action. The hand is too strong to fold but does not benefit enough from raising.

Avoid: Folding is far too tight, while raising isolates too often against straights and stronger value hands.

solutions.name: auto
solutions.message:
Hero: Eychenne calls with two pair, keeping bluffs in and avoiding a huge pot on a very connected board.

Solver: Call is the highest-EV action. The hand is too strong to fold but does not benefit enough from raising.

Avoid: Folding is far too tight, while raising isolates too often against straights and stronger value hands.

solutions.name: pro
solutions.message:
Hero: Eychenne folds against a large polarized river bet on a paired board, likely expecting Zaffagnini to be underbluffing under final-table pressure.

Solver: Call is the highest-EV action. T8 blocks full houses with TT and 88 while leaving missed club draws available as natural bluffs.

Avoid: Raising is too ambitious because better hands continue, while folding gives up one of the strongest bluff-catchers in the range.
